headline: Sarawak Demands 20% Oil Royalty Up From Current 5%
context: Sarawak Premier Abang Johari reiterated demands for a 20% petroleum royalty in January 2026, threatening to withhold state GPS support for the federal coalition if Putrajaya does not honour the Malaysia Agreement 1963 terms.

cards.big: Sarawak produces 48% of Malaysia's oil but receives only 5% royalty — a RM15 billion annual difference at 20%
cards.sub: MA63 promised Sabah and Sarawak resource autonomy — 63 years later, Putrajaya still controls the revenue

cards.big: At 20% royalty, Sarawak would receive RM18 billion annually versus current RM3.2 billion — transformative for state development
cards.sub: Sarawak's GDP per capita is 22% below Selangor despite producing nearly half of national petroleum

cards.big: 71% of Sarawak's rural communities lack piped water — federal underfunding despite resource extraction is the direct cause
cards.sub: Infrastructure gap between Peninsula and Borneo states has widened every decade since formation of Malaysia

cards.big: Petroleum Development Act 1974 vested all oil rights in Petronas — effectively overriding MA63 resource provisions
cards.sub: Legal challenge by Sarawak in 2019 was settled politically without court ruling on constitutional ownership

cards.big: Extracting Sarawak's oil while its villages lack clean water isn't national unity — it's internal colonialism by another name
Should petroleum revenue sharing be renegotiated under a new MA63 implementation framework with binding arbitration?
